title: Zapiekanka
subtitle: Polish open mushroom and cheese baguette
excerpt: A toasted baguette half piled with fried mushrooms and melted cheese, finished with ketchup and pickled cucumber. Poland's most popular street food, sold from milk bars and street kiosks since the communist era.
sourceNotes: Zapiekanka became one of the most recognisable Polish street foods during the communist period, when it was sold from milk bars (bary mleczne) and food kiosks as a cheap hot snack. The name comes from the Polish word for 'baked' or 'gratin'. It has experienced a revival in modern Polish cities, where specialist zapiekanka stalls now offer elaborate toppings, but the classic version remains the most popular: mushrooms, cheese, and ketchup on a long white baguette.

content.text: Zapiekanka is a simple object: a long baguette half, cut side up, loaded with mushrooms fried with onion and thyme, covered with cheese, and put under the grill until the cheese bubbles and browns. Ketchup goes on at the end, in a zigzag pattern across the top. In Poland it is eaten standing up, folded in a paper bag.

The mushrooms must be cooked down properly before they go on the bread, wet mushrooms make the bread soggy. Fry them over high heat until all the liquid has evaporated and they are starting to catch at the edges.

Heat the {{unsalted-butter}} in a frying pan over high heat. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 4 minutes until softened. Add the {{mushrooms-button}} and {{garlic}} and cook without stirring for 2 minutes, then stir and continue frying for 5 to 6 minutes until all the moisture has cooked off and the mushrooms are beginning to brown. Add the {{thyme}}, season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper-ground}}, and stir. Remove from the heat.

Heat the grill to high. Place the baguette halves cut-side up on a baking tray. If using tomato puree, spread a thin layer over each half. Divide the mushroom mixture between the halves and spread evenly. Cover with the {{gruyere}}. Grill for 3 to 4 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling with dark patches. Serve immediately.

items.fix: Return the mushrooms to the pan and cook over high heat until any remaining liquid is gone. Blot with kitchen paper if needed before loading the bread
items.cause: Mushrooms not cooked dry before going on the bread

items.fix: Use the highest grill setting and move the tray to the top shelf. A thinner layer of cheese browns faster and more evenly
items.cause: Grill not hot enough or cheese too thick

items.fix: Move the tray one shelf lower. The bread should toast lightly while the cheese melts above it, this takes 3 to 4 minutes at the right distance
items.cause: Too close to the grill, or grill too hot

content.text: Zapiekanka was the fast food of communist Poland. It was cheap, hot, and sold from the state-run milk bars in every town. After 1989 it fell out of fashion when foreign chains arrived. Then it quietly came back. Kraków's Kazimierz district now has stalls selling fancy versions with pulled pork, truffle oil, and twenty toppings. The mushroom and cheese version is still the one most Poles picture when they hear the word.
